title: Spotify’s AI-powered DJ feature adds four more languages - Music Ally
text:
Spotify has some new recruits to its music-programming team. Maïa, Ben, Alex and Dani began work this week, although they won’t be seen gossiping round the water-cooler any time soon.
They’re the latest versions of Spotify’s AI-powered ‘DJ’ feature
, which has expanded with four new personas and languages: French, German, Italian and Brazilian Portuguese respectively.
The additions have fuelled a geographic expansion for the feature, which is now available – still in beta – in Austria, Brazil, France, Germany, Italy, Portugal, South Korea and Switzerland among other countries. More than 75 in total now.
DJ was already reaching plenty of ears around the world.
In Spotify’s recent financial-earnings call
, co-CEO Gustav Söderström said that the feature is “now used by 94 million subscribers, closing in on 100 million, driving billions of hours of engagement”.
That meant it was being used by nearly a third of Spotify’s 293 million global subscribers even before the latest expansion.
In other, more-niche (for now) news, Spotify is also making it easier for people using AI agents including OpenClaw, Claude Code or OpenAI Codex to
save personalised podcasts created by those tools to their Spotify libraries
.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
